name: Mary Mary
description:
'''Mary Mary''' is an American female gospel music duo formed in [[Inglewood, California]], in 1998. The members are composed of sisters [[Erica Campbell|Erica Atkins-Campbell]] and [[Tina Campbell|Tina Atkins-Campbell]].

Mary Mary first performed in local churches and gospel competitions in California and later as backup singers for several [[R&B]] acts. After signing to [[Columbia Records]] in 1999, they released their debut album ''[[Thankful (Mary Mary album)|Thankful]]'' (2000), which sold over two million copies in the United States and also earned a [[Grammy Award for Best Contemporary R&B Gospel Album]] at the [[43rd Annual Grammy Awards]] (2001). The album spawned the international chart-topping single "[[Shackles (Praise You)]]". They released two studio albums between 2002 and 2005, including the successful albums ''[[Incredible (Mary Mary album)|Incredible]]'' (2002) and ''[[Mary Mary (album)|Mary Mary]]'' (2005) and singles "In the Morning" (2002), "Heaven" (2005), and "Yesterday" (2005). After releasing the Christmas album ''[[A Mary Mary Christmas]]'' (2006), they served as judges on the television singing competition series ''[[Sunday Best (American TV series)|Sunday Best]]'' (2007–2011).

After a brief hiatus, Mary Mary released their fifth album ''[[The Sound (Mary Mary album)|The Sound]]'' (2008), which produced the US Gospel top-three singles "[[Get Up (Mary Mary song)|Get Up]]" and "[[God in Me]]", with the latter peaking atop of the chart. In 2012, Mary Mary went on hiatus to pursue solo careers, following the release of their compilation album ''[[Go Get It (album)|Go Get It]]''. They starred in their [[We TV]] reality television series ''[[Mary Mary (TV series)|Mary Mary]]'' (2012–2017), which followed their solo careers and personal lives.

Mary Mary is one of the [[List of best-selling gospel music artists|best-selling gospel music acts of all time]], with estimated sales of over 8 million records. They have received [[List of awards and nominations received by Mary Mary|many accolades]], including four [[Grammy Awards]], seventeen [[Stellar Awards]], seven [[GMA Dove Awards]], and two [[American Music Awards]]. In 2025, they were inducted into the Missouri Gospel Music Hall of Fame.
